response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong web and full-stack engineer with impressive high-throughput systems experience, but the role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise as its core requirement. His only documented C++ work is a six-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and C++ does not even appear in his listed skills section. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 security standards, multi-threaded systems programming, message-broker architecture in C++, or any of the industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) the role lists as advantageous. His career trajectory is firmly web-oriented, making this a poor match for a senior C++ platform engineering role in industrial automation.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-performance, high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic) showing systems-level thinking
Event-driven and geo-data platform architecture experience aligns loosely with IoT and distributed-systems concepts mentioned in the JD
Broad full-stack versatility (Node.js, TypeScript, React) matches several advantageous skills in the JD
concerns: C++ experience is limited to roughly six months in 2014–2015 and is absent from his own skills list — the primary technical requirement of the role is effectively unmet
No industrial automation domain knowledge and no exposure to IEC 62443, Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, or any of the industrial/embedded protocols the role requires
Education is a BS in Computer Science and a Fine Arts degree, neither matching the B.E./B.Tech engineering profile specified, and no secure development lifecycle or SDL experience is evident

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 background
